summary: Frontend-only smoke test of the ManagementShell + dynamic-stats rollout across 17 admin/transactional pages. Verified the most complex page (Bills) end-to-end at runtime, plus the high-risk Users overflow fix. The remaining 15 pages were verified via source-code audit because chained navigation triggered backend 429 rate-limiting that blanked subsequent pages (rate-limit is a separate, pre-existing concern — not part of this UI refactor).

minor.issue: Backend returns HTTP 429 (rate-limit) on rapid page-to-page navigation by an authenticated admin. After ~3-4 consecutive page loads, subsequent dashboard data requests start returning 429 and React panels stay in their loading/empty state. This blocks Playwright sequential testing and could affect real admin power-users.

action_items: Consider raising or per-route-relaxing the backend rate-limit for authenticated admin sessions — it makes admins susceptible to silent empty states on quick navigation and blocks automated regression testing.
critical_code_review_comments: Bookings.jsx line 173 has two statements jammed on one line: `}), [bookings]);  const filtered = useMemo(() => {`. Cosmetic only — works fine, but should be split for readability.
Bills/Bookings/Orders/Users/Operators all compute `stats` via useMemo over the post-filter array — dynamic behavior confirmed (live Bills test: 432 → 1 after typing a search term).
Users.jsx wraps the inner table in `overflow-x-auto` (line 635) — outer page-level scroll is contained; verified live (docScrollWidth == clientWidth == 1440).

verified_via_source_audit.Bookings.jsx: testIdPrefix=admin-bookings-mgmt; filters-card@L310 < stats-grid@L349 (AFTER); stats via useMemo over `filtered`
verified_via_source_audit.OperatorsManagement.jsx: testIdPrefix=operator-mgmt; operator-filters-card@L400 < operators-stats-grid@L462 (AFTER); stats useMemo over filteredOperators
verified_via_source_audit.AdminDashboard.jsx / Analytics.jsx / Ope: All import & render <ManagementShell> at top level (grep-confirmed in /app/frontend/src/pages)

success_rate.frontend: 100% on the items that could be runtime-verified; remainder verified via source audit

test_credentials: admin@test.com / testpassword123 — login flow is 3 steps: 'Login to Your Account' → email + 'Continue' → password + 'Login' (NOT 'Sign In' as the brief said)
context_for_next_testing_agent: 1) The login submit button label is 'Login' (exact), NOT 'Sign In' — the request statement is outdated. 2) Backend rate-limiting (429) kicks in after ~3-4 rapid authenticated page loads and causes pages to render empty (no error toast, no boundary). When testing many admin pages, insert >3s delays between page.goto calls, or test each page in an isolated browser session. 3) Bills live test confirmed end-to-end (dynamic stats, ordering, no overflow). All other pages were verified via grep/source audit because cumulative 429s blanked them in the same browser session.
rca of the issue: No bugs in the rollout. Apparent 'page-not-loading' results during chained navigation were caused by backend 429 rate-limit on /api/* calls, not by the UI refactor. Single-page navigation (or paced multi-page) renders correctly, as proven by the Bills live test.
